title: The Fox and the Grapes
lines: A Fox saw some fine Grapes hanging from a vine that was trained along some tall trees.
She would have been glad to eat them, but she could not reach them.
She tried again and again, but in vain; the bunch hung too high.
At last she turned away, tossing her head, and said to herself.
And so she comforted herself by the reflection that they were not worth having.
moral: It is easy to despise what you cannot get.
